title: readiness probe for play kube
body:
/kind feature

In pod spec as defined by Kuberenetes there is [readiness](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/#define-readiness-probes) probe. ATM the support is missing in podman play kube.

comments.body:
@pkliczewski I worked on liveness probe a while ago since we dont have `kubelet` here it was backed by native podman health checks / systemd and restarts container if they need to. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/10956

I might be missing on something but i am still curious what practical application would `readiness` probe would solve here since we don't have a construct of `service` in podman and there is no loadbalancer which will pass traffic to only `ready` pods.
When multiple containers are booting and some depend on others with readiness probe it would be easy to understand whether application would work as a whole.
This doesn't sound like a pod state to me - possibly a bool in `podman pod inspect` that states if the probes are healthy (`Ready: true` or `Ready: false`).

> need another pod field in podman.

@mheon Yes I intended the same a new field other than `pod state`. I guess it was not clear enough. K8s has a seperate `Ready` field other than Pod state.
@umohnani8 FYI
If Podman play kube launched a pod that required another pod to be ready, would it wait for the readiness probe to complete?

> If Podman play kube launched a pod that required another pod to be ready, would it wait for the readiness probe to complete?

It depends whether the readiness probe is used by podman or by a pod waiting on required dependency. I think it should be the latter.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
